name: web_scraper
description: Fetches and extracts readable text or structured content from a webpage. Without a selector it returns the page title, main text, and links. With a CSS selector it can extract text, attributes, rows, or tables. Use this to read web pages, documentation, articles, or extract structured data like product lists.

parameters.url: The full URL of the webpage to scrape (must start with http:// or https://)
parameters.mode: Optional scraping mode: auto, static, dynamic, or rss. Use rss for RSS/Atom feeds and dynamic for JavaScript-rendered pages.
parameters.wait_for_selector: Optional CSS selector to wait for in dynamic mode or auto dynamic fallback.
parameters.selector: Optional CSS selector to extract matching element(s). When omitted, the full readable page is returned.
parameters.fields: Optional object mapping field names to CSS selectors (relative to selector) for structured row extraction. Append @attr to read an attribute, e.g. {"link": "a@href"}.
parameters.output_format: Optional output shape when selector is set: auto (default), text, html, rows, table. Auto picks rows if fields is set, list if attribute is set, otherwise text.
parameters.attribute: Optional attribute name to extract when output_format is list, e.g. href or src.
parameters.limit: Optional maximum number of matches to return when selector is set (1-1000, default: 50).
parameters.search_query: When summary mode is active and no selector is set, tell the summariser what specific information to extract from the page. Be specific.

returns: Extracted text content of the page, or structured matches/rows/table when a selector is used
